pi_items.source: Task description and pattern consistent with other DreamPlan tasks (e.g., complete_home_staging stores files in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\). Path follows Windows standard Documents folder convention.
verifier.py line 73: checks 'if file_size > 5000' bytes (5KB threshold), contradicting the metadata claim of 10KB minimum

1) DreamPlan software by NCH Software (https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/) supports plumbing fixtures and bathroom design 2) verifier.py lines 93-95 explicitly checks for 'Bathtub', 'Toilet', and 'Bathroom Sink or Vanity' 3) task.json description lists these three items as requirements
verifier.py lines 34-39: default score_weights matches metadata exactly. Used in line 64: score += score_weights['file_exists']
verifier.py lines 34-39: default score_weights matches metadata exactly. Used in lines 68 and 74: score += score_weights['file_valid'] / 2
verifier.py lines 34-39: default score_weights matches metadata exactly. Used in line 119: content_score = (items_found / 3) * score_weights['vlm_content']
verifier.py lines 34-39: default score_weights matches metadata exactly. Used in line 157: workflow_score += score_weights['vlm_workflow']
env.json description and tags mention 'nch-software'. Web search confirms NCH Software develops DreamPlan Home Design Software (https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/)
env.json description: 'NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software for architectural design tasks'. Multiple web sources confirm this is the official name.

privileged_info_summary: DreamPlan Home Design Software (NCH Software) running on Windows 11 supports the required bathroom design task. The task requires placing three real bathroom fixtures: Bathtub, Toilet, and Sink/Vanity. These fixtures are documented features of DreamPlan software. The scoring system weights file verification at 20 points, VLM content analysis at 60 points, and workflow verification at 20 points. However, there is a discrepancy: the metadata claims a 10KB minimum file size requirement, but the verifier code actually uses a 5KB threshold. The expected output path (C:\Users\Docker\Documents\bathroom_design.png) follows the Windows Documents folder convention and is consistent with other task patterns. The required PowerShell setup and export scripts referenced in task.json do not currently exist in the task directory, indicating the task is incomplete.
